file.content:
# Archive - Historical Reference Files

**Last Updated:** November 19, 2025
**Purpose:** Preserve historical brainstorming, exploration, and superseded documentation for reference

---

## Important Context

### Single Source of Truth

**`docs/` = CURRENT & AUTHORITATIVE**
- All files in `docs/` are the most recent, actively maintained official documentation
- This is the single source of truth for the project
- Last updated: November 13-14, 2025

**`.archive/` = HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY**
- Files here are for historical context and inspiration
- Some content has been extracted and integrated into current docs
- These files may contain outdated information or alternative product visions
- **Do not use as primary reference** - always check `docs/` first

---

## Directory Structure

### `completed-tasks/`

**Purpose:** Archive for completed task planning documents from `tasks/` directory

**Archival Process:**
- When a task is 100% complete and committed to git, move task file here
- Preserves execution history and lessons learned
- Keeps active `tasks/` directory clean and focused

**Naming Convention:**
- Preserve original filename: `[feature-name].md`
- Task files include completion date in review section

**Contents:**
- **59 completed task files** from `tasks/` directory (as of November 19, 2025)
- Each file contains: overview, plan, checklist, and comprehensive review section
- All tasks include **Created:** and **Completed:** dates based on git history

**Why Archive Tasks:**
- Keeps `tasks/` directory clean (only active/upcoming work)
- Preserves execution history and lessons learned
- Searchable reference for "how we solved X before"
- Tracks actual time spent vs estimates for future planning

---

### `gemini-analysis-2025-11-19/`

**Source:** Gemini 3.0 Pro Agentic AI codebase analysis session (November 19, 2025)

**Contents:** Gemini-generated documentation files and analysis

**Files:**
- `GEMINI.md` - Gemini's interpretation of CLAUDE.md (simplified, 6x shorter)
- `sprint4-notes-mvp-plan.md` - Sprint 4 Notes MVP plan (archived as completed)
- `gemini/` directory:
  - `api_integration_guide.md` - AI provider integration guide
  - `widget_development_guide.md` - Widget development guide
  - `bug_report_2025_11_19.md` - Codebase audit bug report
  - `feature_gap_analysis.md` - Missing features analysis
  - `archival_plan.md` - Documentation cleanup recommendations

**Status:**
- ✅ **All Gemini docs validated** against actual codebase - 100% accurate
- ✅ **Bug reports confirmed** - All TODOs exist exactly where reported
- ✅ **API Integration Guide** - Extracted and integrated into `docs/technical/ai_provider_integration.md`
- ✅ **Widget Development Guide** - Extracted and integrated into `docs/technical/widget_development.md`
- ✅ **Feature Gap Analysis** - Insights integrated into `docs/product/feature_roadmap.md`
- ❌ **GEMINI.md** - Not integrated (CLAUDE.md is canonical, GEMINI.md adds no new value)
- ✅ **Sprint 4 plan** - Archived (Sprint 4 Notes MVP completed, plan outdated)

**Why Archived:**
Gemini provided excellent analysis and accurate bug reports. The valuable content (API guide, widget guide, feature gaps) has been extracted and integrated into official documentation. GEMINI.md is superseded by the more comprehensive CLAUDE.md. Sprint 4 plan is archived as the work is complete.

---

### `chatgpt-brainstorming/`

**Source:** `.notes/ChatGPT (Inspiration Reference Only)`

**Contents:** ChatGPT brainstorming sessions and RFC specifications from November 2025

**Files:**
- `neuman-brain-automations-rfc-20251112-023402.md` - Automation engine specification
- `neuman-brain-import-export-testkit-20251112-023402/` - Import/export test data

**Status:**
- ✅ **Automation RFC** extracted and integrated into `docs/technical/automation_system.md`
  - Complete automation engine specification (~7,500 words)
  - Trigger → Condition → Action model
  - JSON schemas, safety mechanisms, example automations
- ✅ **Import/Export specs** extracted and integrated into `docs/technical/import_export_spec.md`
  - Complete data portability specification (~6,000 words)
  - Export formats (.brain, CSV, ICS)
  - Import formats (Trello, Asana, Generic CSV, ICS)
  - Dry-run preview system
  - Test data preserved in archive for reference

**Why Archived:**
These brainstorming sessions were valuable for extracting technical specifications, but the content is now properly integrated into official documentation. The files are preserved here for historical reference and to track the evolution of ideas.

---

### `claude-exploration/`

**Source:** `.notes/Claude (Inspiration Reference Only)`

**Contents:** Claude AI exploration sessions exploring alternative product visions (October-November 2025)

**Files:**
- 12 comprehensive exploration documents covering:
  - Personal Knowledge Management (PKM) vision
  - Note-taking and AI features
  - Graph view and advanced search
  - Real-time collaboration
  - Visual planning tools
  - Alternative feature prioritizations

**CRITICAL CONTEXT:**
⚠️ **These files represent a DIFFERENT product vision than the current direction.**

**Current Vision (docs/):**
- All-in-one productivity dashboard
- Habit tracking + time tracking + tasks + calendar + gamification
- Beautiful bento-card UI
- Privacy-first, local-first architecture
- Q1 2026 focus: IndexedDB migration, Notes MVP, File System Access API

**Claude Exploration Vision (archived):**
- Personal Knowledge Management (PKM) focus
- Note-taking with AI features as primary value proposition
- Graph view, backlinks, advanced search as core features
- Different feature prioritization and roadmap

**Why These Visions Differ:**
During exploration sessions, Claude investigated alternative approaches to building a "Second Brain" product. While the PKM/notes-first approach is valid and explored in depth, the current product direction focuses on productivity dashboard with habit tracking as the primary differentiator.

**Status:**
- ❌ **NOT integrated** into current docs (represents alternative product direction)
- ℹ️ **Preserved for reference** - these explorations contain valuable insights about PKM features, AI integration, and graph databases that may be relevant for future feature development (Q2-Q4 2026)

**Valuable Content to Consider Later:**
- Graph view architecture (Cytoscape.js, D3.js, force-directed layouts)
- Advanced search with semantic search and fuzzy matching
- AI features (auto-tagging, smart suggestions, content generation)
- Backlink system design
- Real-time collaboration architecture
- Visual planning tools (mind maps, flowcharts)

**Why Archived:**
These explorations were valuable for understanding different product approaches, but they don't align with the current product vision. They're preserved here in case we want to revisit PKM features in the future (e.g., Q3-Q4 2026 after core dashboard is mature).

---

### `superseded-docs/`

**Source:** `.notes/Official Documentation`

**Contents:** Old official documentation (pre-November 2025 reorganization)

**Files:**
- Old competitive analysis
- Old feature specifications (Gantt charts, time tracking)
- Outdated Firebase/Google Drive architecture docs
- Historical roadmaps and feature lists

**Status:**
- ✅ **Competitive analysis** - Detailed competitor feature matrices extracted and integrated into `docs/strategy/competitive_landscape.md`
  - Added executive summary with market gap analysis
  - Added complete feature comparison matrices (20 features × 5 competitors)
  - Added habit & productivity apps comparison
  - Added competitive scoring, positioning statements, threat analysis
  - Enhanced from 217 lines to ~375 lines
- ⚠️ **Firebase/Google Drive docs** - OUTDATED (Firebase removed in Sprint 2, Week 3-4)
- ℹ️ **Feature specs** - Some specs (Gantt, time tracking) may have valuable details not yet in current docs
  - Consider extracting to `docs/technical/` if implementing these features

**Why Archived:**
These were the official docs before the November 2025 comprehensive documentation rewrite. The competitive analysis had valuable detailed competitor matrices that were integrated into current docs. Other content is outdated (Firebase) or may be valuable for future feature implementation.

---

### `examples/`

**Source:** `.notes/Examples`

**Contents:** Example files, test data, and reference materials

**Files:**
- Sample task exports
- Example automation rules
- Reference screenshots
- Test data sets

**Status:**
- ℹ️ **Preserved as-is** - Examples are useful for testing and reference

**Why Archived:**
These examples are valuable for testing import/export functionality and for reference when implementing features. They're not part of official documentation but are useful to keep around.

---

## What Was Extracted and Integrated

### From ChatGPT Brainstorming:

**Created `docs/technical/automation_system.md`** (~7,500 words)
- Complete automation engine specification
- Data model with TypeScript interfaces and JSON Schema
- Trigger types: onCreate, onUpdate, onMove, onDue, cron
- Action types: set, move, addTag, create, notify, snooze
- Safety mechanisms: loop prevention, rate limiting, idempotency
- IndexedDB storage and Service Worker integration
- 5 detailed example automations

**Created `docs/technical/import_export_spec.md`** (~6,000 words)
- Complete data portability specification
- Export formats: JSON Bundle (.brain), CSV, ICS
- Import formats: .brain, Trello JSON, Asana CSV, Generic CSV, ICS
- Dry-run preview system with conflict resolution
- Field mapping UI specification
- Lossless round-trip guarantees

### From Superseded Docs:

**Enhanced `docs/strategy/competitive_landscape.md`** (~375 lines, enhanced from 217)
- Added executive summary with market gap analysis
- Added complete feature comparison matrices (All-in-One platforms)
- Added habit & productivity apps comparison table
- Added competitive scoring summary (current 4.5/10 → target 9.5/10 by Q4 2026)
- Added strategic recommendations by sprint
- Added positioning statements for marketing
- Added threat analysis (High/Medium/Low threats with mitigations)

### From Claude Exploration:

**Nothing integrated** - Represents alternative product vision (PKM/notes-first vs current productivity dashboard)
- Preserved for future reference when implementing:
  - Graph view (Q3 2026)
  - Advanced search (Q3 2026)
  - AI features (Q4 2026+)
  - Real-time collaboration (Q2 2026)

---

## How to Use This Archive

### When to Reference Archive Files:

1. **Implementing automations** → Check `chatgpt-brainstorming/neuman-brain-automations-rfc-*`
2. **Implementing import/export** → Check test data in `chatgpt-brainstorming/neuman-brain-import-export-testkit-*`
3. **Implementing PKM features** (Q3-Q4 2026) → Check `claude-exploration/` for graph view, AI features
4. **Competitive analysis deep-dive** → Check `superseded-docs/` for detailed competitor feature matrices (already integrated into current docs)
5. **Testing with example data** → Check `examples/`

### When NOT to Use Archive Files:

1. **Looking for current project information** → Always use `docs/` first
2. **Understanding current product vision** → Use `docs/product/product_vision.md` (NOT Claude exploration files)
3. **Current architecture** → Use `docs/technical/` (NOT superseded Firebase docs)
4. **Current roadmap** → Use `docs/product/feature_roadmap.md` (NOT archived roadmaps)

---

## File Preservation Philosophy

**Why archive instead of delete?**

1. **Historical context** - Track evolution of ideas and product decisions
2. **Future reference** - PKM features, AI integration, graph databases may be relevant in Q3-Q4 2026
3. **Test data** - Import/export test data valuable for implementation
4. **Learning** - See what approaches were explored and why they weren't chosen
5. **Reversibility** - If we change direction, we have the context

**Storage cost:** Minimal (~2MB total) - archive costs are negligible compared to value of historical context

---

## Maintenance

### When to Add to Archive:

- Old official docs that are superseded by new comprehensive docs
- Brainstorming sessions that produce extracted specifications
- Alternative product vision explorations
- Historical roadmaps and feature lists (after major pivots)

### When to Clean Archive:

- **Never delete without review** - storage is cheap, context is valuable
- Consider moving to `archive/deprecated/` if truly obsolete
- Update this README when adding new categories

### Archive Organization:

```
.archive/
├── README.md                          # This file
├── gemini-analysis-2025-11-19/       # Gemini codebase analysis (guides extracted to docs/technical/)
├── chatgpt-brainstorming/            # ChatGPT RFC specs (extracted to docs/technical/)
├── claude-exploration/               # Alternative PKM vision (preserved for Q3-Q4 2026)
├── superseded-docs/                  # Old official docs (competitive analysis extracted)
└── examples/                         # Test data and reference materials
```

---

## Questions?

- **"Should I reference archive files?"** → Only for historical context or future features (PKM, AI)
- **"Where's the current info?"** → Always check `docs/` first
- **"Why keep Claude exploration files?"** → Valuable for Q3-Q4 2026 PKM features, even though not current direction
- **"Can I delete .notes/ now?"** → Yes, all content copied to .archive/

---

**Last Review:** November 19, 2025 (Task cleanup: 59 completed tasks archived, 44 active tasks remain)
**Next Review:** When adding major new archive content or implementing archived features (Q2-Q4 2026)

- All files in `docs/` are the most recent, actively maintained official documentation

- These files may contain outdated information or alternative product visions
- **Do not use as primary reference** - always check `docs/` first

---

## Directory Structure

### `completed-tasks/`

**Purpose:** Archive for completed task planning documents from `tasks/` directory

**Archival Process:**
- When a task is 100% complete and committed to git, move task file here
- Preserves execution history and lessons learned
- Keeps active `tasks/` directory clean and focused

**Naming Convention:**
- Preserve original filename: `[feature-name].md`
- Task files include completion date in review section

**Contents:**
- **59 completed task files** from `tasks/` directory (as of November 19, 2025)
- Each file contains: overview, plan, checklist, and comprehensive review section
- All tasks include **Created:** and **Completed:** dates based on git history

**Why Archive Tasks:**
- Keeps `tasks/` directory clean (only active/upcoming work)
- Preserves execution history and lessons learned
- Searchable reference for "how we solved X before"
- Tracks actual time spent vs estimates for future planning

---

### `gemini-analysis-2025-11-19/`

**Source:** Gemini 3.0 Pro Agentic AI codebase analysis session (November 19, 2025)

**Contents:** Gemini-generated documentation files and analysis

**Files:**
- `GEMINI.md` - Gemini's interpretation of CLAUDE.md (simplified, 6x shorter)
- `sprint4-notes-mvp-plan.md` - Sprint 4 Notes MVP plan (archived as completed)
- `gemini/` directory:
  - `api_integration_guide.md` - AI provider integration guide
  - `widget_development_guide.md` - Widget development guide
  - `bug_report_2025_11_19.md` - Codebase audit bug report
  - `feature_gap_analysis.md` - Missing features analysis
  - `archival_plan.md` - Documentation cleanup recommendations

**Status:**
- ✅ **All Gemini docs validated** against actual codebase - 100% accurate
- ✅ **Bug reports confirmed** - All TODOs exist exactly where reported
- ✅ **API Integration Guide** - Extracted and integrated into `docs/technical/ai_provider_integration.md`
- ✅ **Widget Development Guide** - Extracted and integrated into `docs/technical/widget_development.md`
- ✅ **Feature Gap Analysis** - Insights integrated into `docs/product/feature_roadmap.md`
- ❌ **GEMINI.md** - Not integrated (CLAUDE.md is canonical, GEMINI.md adds no new value)
- ✅ **Sprint 4 plan** - Archived (Sprint 4 Notes MVP completed, plan outdated)

**Why Archived:**
Gemini provided excellent analysis and accurate bug reports. The valuable content (API guide, widget guide, feature gaps) has been extracted and integrated into official documentation. GEMINI.md is superseded by the more comprehensive CLAUDE.md. Sprint 4 plan is archived as the work is complete.

---

### `chatgpt-brainstorming/`

**Source:** `.notes/ChatGPT (Inspiration Reference Only)`

**Contents:** ChatGPT brainstorming sessions and RFC specifications from November 2025

**Files:**
- `neuman-brain-automations-rfc-20251112-023402.md` - Automation engine specification
- `neuman-brain-import-export-testkit-20251112-023402/` - Import/export test data

**Status:**
- ✅ **Automation RFC** extracted and integrated into `docs/technical/automation_system.md`
  - Complete automation engine specification (~7,500 words)
  - Trigger → Condition → Action model
  - JSON schemas, safety mechanisms, example automations
- ✅ **Import/Export specs** extracted and integrated into `docs/technical/import_export_spec.md`
  - Complete data portability specification (~6,000 words)
  - Export formats (.brain, CSV, ICS)
  - Import formats (Trello, Asana, Generic CSV, ICS)
  - Dry-run preview system
  - Test data preserved in archive for reference

**Why Archived:**
These brainstorming sessions were valuable for extracting technical specifications, but the content is now properly integrated into official documentation. The files are preserved here for historical reference and to track the evolution of ideas.

---

### `claude-exploration/`

**Source:** `.notes/Claude (Inspiration Reference Only)`

**Contents:** Claude AI exploration sessions exploring alternative product visions (October-November 2025)

**Files:**
- 12 comprehensive exploration documents covering:
  - Personal Knowledge Management (PKM) vision
  - Note-taking and AI features
  - Graph view and advanced search
  - Real-time collaboration
  - Visual planning tools
  - Alternative feature prioritizations

**CRITICAL CONTEXT:**
⚠️ **These files represent a DIFFERENT product vision than the current direction.**

**Current Vision (docs/):**
- All-in-one productivity dashboard
- Habit tracking + time tracking + tasks + calendar + gamification
- Beautiful bento-card UI
- Privacy-first, local-first architecture
- Q1 2026 focus: IndexedDB migration, Notes MVP, File System Access API

**Claude Exploration Vision (archived):**
- Personal Knowledge Management (PKM) focus
- Note-taking with AI features as primary value proposition
- Graph view, backlinks, advanced search as core features
- Different feature prioritization and roadmap

**Why These Visions Differ:**
During exploration sessions, Claude investigated alternative approaches to building a "Second Brain" product. While the PKM/notes-first approach is valid and explored in depth, the current product direction focuses on productivity dashboard with habit tracking as the primary differentiator.

**Status:**
- ❌ **NOT integrated** into current docs (represents alternative product direction)
- ℹ️ **Preserved for reference** - these explorations contain valuable insights about PKM features, AI integration, and graph databases that may be relevant for future feature development (Q2-Q4 2026)

**Valuable Content to Consider Later:**
- Graph view architecture (Cytoscape.js, D3.js, force-directed layouts)
- Advanced search with semantic search and fuzzy matching
- AI features (auto-tagging, smart suggestions, content generation)
- Backlink system design
- Real-time collaboration architecture
- Visual planning tools (mind maps, flowcharts)

**Why Archived:**
These explorations were valuable for understanding different product approaches, but they don't align with the current product vision. They're preserved here in case we want to revisit PKM features in the future (e.g., Q3-Q4 2026 after core dashboard is mature).

---

### `superseded-docs/`

**Source:** `.notes/Official Documentation`

**Contents:** Old official documentation (pre-November 2025 reorganization)

**Files:**
- Old competitive analysis
- Old feature specifications (Gantt charts, time tracking)
- Outdated Firebase/Google Drive architecture docs
- Historical roadmaps and feature lists

**Status:**
- ✅ **Competitive analysis** - Detailed competitor feature matrices extracted and integrated into `docs/strategy/competitive_landscape.md`
  - Added executive summary with market gap analysis
  - Added complete feature comparison matrices (20 features × 5 competitors)
  - Added habit & productivity apps comparison
  - Added competitive scoring, positioning statements, threat analysis
  - Enhanced from 217 lines to ~375 lines
- ⚠️ **Firebase/Google Drive docs** - OUTDATED (Firebase removed in Sprint 2, Week 3-4)
- ℹ️ **Feature specs** - Some specs (Gantt, time tracking) may have valuable details not yet in current docs
  - Consider extracting to `docs/technical/` if implementing these features

**Why Archived:**
These were the official docs before the November 2025 comprehensive documentation rewrite. The competitive analysis had valuable detailed competitor matrices that were integrated into current docs. Other content is outdated (Firebase) or may be valuable for future feature implementation.

---

### `examples/`

**Source:** `.notes/Examples`

**Contents:** Example files, test data, and reference materials

**Files:**
- Sample task exports
- Example automation rules
- Reference screenshots
- Test data sets

**Status:**
- ℹ️ **Preserved as-is** - Examples are useful for testing and reference

**Why Archived:**
These examples are valuable for testing import/export functionality and for reference when implementing features. They're not part of official documentation but are useful to keep around.

---

## What Was Extracted and Integrated

### From ChatGPT Brainstorming:

**Created `docs/technical/automation_system.md`** (~7,500 words)
- Complete automation engine specification
- Data model with TypeScript interfaces and JSON Schema
- Trigger types: onCreate, onUpdate, onMove, onDue, cron
- Action types: set, move, addTag, create, notify, snooze
- Safety mechanisms: loop prevention, rate limiting, idempotency
- IndexedDB storage and Service Worker integration
- 5 detailed example automations

**Created `docs/technical/import_export_spec.md`** (~6,000 words)
- Complete data portability specification
- Export formats: JSON Bundle (.brain), CSV, ICS
- Import formats: .brain, Trello JSON, Asana CSV, Generic CSV, ICS
- Dry-run preview system with conflict resolution
- Field mapping UI specification
- Lossless round-trip guarantees

### From Superseded Docs:

**Enhanced `docs/strategy/competitive_landscape.md`** (~375 lines, enhanced from 217)
- Added executive summary with market gap analysis
- Added complete feature comparison matrices (All-in-One platforms)
- Added habit & productivity apps comparison table
- Added competitive scoring summary (current 4.5/10 → target 9.5/10 by Q4 2026)
- Added strategic recommendations by sprint
- Added positioning statements for marketing
- Added threat analysis (High/Medium/Low threats with mitigations)

### From Claude Exploration:

**Nothing integrated** - Represents alternative product vision (PKM/notes-first vs current productivity dashboard)
- Preserved for future reference when implementing:
  - Graph view (Q3 2026)
  - Advanced search (Q3 2026)
  - AI features (Q4 2026+)
  - Real-time collaboration (Q2 2026)

---

## How to Use This Archive

### When to Reference Archive Files:

1. **Implementing automations** → Check `chatgpt-brainstorming/neuman-brain-automations-rfc-*`
2. **Implementing import/export** → Check test data in `chatgpt-brainstorming/neuman-brain-import-export-testkit-*`
3. **Implementing PKM features** (Q3-Q4 2026) → Check `claude-exploration/` for graph view, AI features
4. **Competitive analysis deep-dive** → Check `superseded-docs/` for detailed competitor feature matrices (already integrated into current docs)
5. **Testing with example data** → Check `examples/`

### When NOT to Use Archive Files:

1. **Looking for current project information** → Always use `docs/` first
2. **Understanding current product vision** → Use `docs/product/product_vision.md` (NOT Claude exploration files)
3. **Current architecture** → Use `docs/technical/` (NOT superseded Firebase docs)
4. **Current roadmap** → Use `docs/product/feature_roadmap.md` (NOT archived roadmaps)

---

## File Preservation Philosophy

**Why archive instead of delete?**

1. **Historical context** - Track evolution of ideas and product decisions
2. **Future reference** - PKM features, AI integration, graph databases may be relevant in Q3-Q4 2026
3. **Test data** - Import/export test data valuable for implementation
4. **Learning** - See what approaches were explored and why they weren't chosen
5. **Reversibility** - If we change direction, we have the context

**Storage cost:** Minimal (~2MB total) - archive costs are negligible compared to value of historical context

---

## Maintenance

### When to Add to Archive:

- Old official docs that are superseded by new comprehensive docs
- Brainstorming sessions that produce extracted specifications
- Alternative product vision explorations
- Historical roadmaps and feature lists (after major pivots)

### When to Clean Archive:

- **Never delete without review** - storage is cheap, context is valuable
- Consider moving to `archive/deprecated/` if truly obsolete
- Update this README when adding new categories

### Archive Organization:

```
.archive/
├── README.md                          # This file
├── gemini-analysis-2025-11-19/       # Gemini codebase analysis (guides extracted to docs/technical/)
├── chatgpt-brainstorming/            # ChatGPT RFC specs (extracted to docs/technical/)
├── claude-exploration/               # Alternative PKM vision (preserved for Q3-Q4 2026)
├── superseded-docs/                  # Old official docs (competitive analysis extracted)
└── examples/                         # Test data and reference materials
```

---

## Questions?

- **"Should I reference archive files?"** → Only for historical context or future features (PKM, AI)
- **"Where's the current info?"** → Always check `docs/` first
- **"Why keep Claude exploration files?"** → Valuable for Q3-Q4 2026 PKM features, even though not current direction
- **"Can I delete .notes/ now?"** → Yes, all content copied to .archive/

---

**Last Review:** November 19, 2025 (Task cleanup: 59 completed tasks archived, 44 active tasks remain)
**Next Review:** When adding major new archive content or implementing archived features (Q2-Q4 2026)

# Archive - Historical Reference Files

**Last Updated:** November 24, 2025
**Purpose:** Preserve historical brainstorming, exploration, superseded documentation, and completed tasks for reference

---

## Important Context

### Single Source of Truth

**`docs/` = CURRENT & AUTHORITATIVE**
- All files in `docs/` are the most recent, actively maintained official documentation
- This is the single source of truth for the project
- Last updated: November 13-14, 2025

**`.archive/` = HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY**
- Files here are for historical context and inspiration
- Some content has been extracted and integrated into current docs
- These files may contain outdated information or alternative product visions
- **Do not use as primary reference** - always check `docs/` first

---

## Directory Structure

### `completed-tasks/`

**Purpose:** Archive for completed task planning documents from `tasks/` directory

**Archival Process:**
- When a task is 100% complete and committed to git, move task file here
- Preserves execution history and lessons learned
- Keeps active `tasks/` directory clean and focused

**Naming Convention:**
- Preserve original filename: `[feature-name].md`
- Task files include completion date in review section

**Contents:**
- **78 completed task files** from `tasks/` directory (as of November 24, 2025)
  - 59 files from November 19, 2025 cleanup
  - 19 additional files from November 24, 2025 cleanup
- Each file contains: overview, plan, checklist, and comprehensive review section
- All tasks include **Created:** and **Completed:** dates based on git history

**Why Archive Tasks:**
- Keeps `tasks/` directory clean (only active/upcoming work)
- Preserves execution history and lessons learned
- Searchable reference for "how we solved X before"
- Tracks actual time spent vs estimates for future planning

---

### `gemini-analysis-2025-11-19/`

**Source:** Gemini 3.0 Pro Agentic AI codebase analysis session (November 19, 2025)

**Contents:** Gemini-generated documentation files and analysis

**Files:**
- `GEMINI.md` - Gemini's interpretation of CLAUDE.md (simplified, 6x shorter)
- `sprint4-notes-mvp-plan.md` - Sprint 4 Notes MVP plan (archived as completed)
- `gemini/` directory:
  - `api_integration_guide.md` - AI provider integration guide
  - `widget_development_guide.md` - Widget development guide
  - `bug_report_2025_11_19.md` - Codebase audit bug report
  - `feature_gap_analysis.md` - Missing features analysis
  - `archival_plan.md` - Documentation cleanup recommendations

**Status:**
- ✅ **All Gemini docs validated** against actual codebase - 100% accurate
- ✅ **Bug reports confirmed** - All TODOs exist exactly where reported
- ✅ **API Integration Guide** - Extracted and integrated into `docs/technical/ai_provider_integration.md`
- ✅ **Widget Development Guide** - Extracted and integrated into `docs/technical/widget_development.md`
- ✅ **Feature Gap Analysis** - Insights integrated into `docs/product/feature_roadmap.md`
- ❌ **GEMINI.md** - Not integrated (CLAUDE.md is canonical, GEMINI.md adds no new value)
- ✅ **Sprint 4 plan** - Archived (Sprint 4 Notes MVP completed, plan outdated)

**Why Archived:**
Gemini provided excellent analysis and accurate bug reports. The valuable content (API guide, widget guide, feature gaps) has been extracted and integrated into official documentation. GEMINI.md is superseded by the more comprehensive CLAUDE.md. Sprint 4 plan is archived as the work is complete.

---

### `chatgpt-brainstorming/`

**Source:** `.notes/ChatGPT (Inspiration Reference Only)`

**Contents:** ChatGPT brainstorming sessions and RFC specifications from November 2025

**Files:**
- `neuman-brain-automations-rfc-20251112-023402.md` - Automation engine specification
- `neuman-brain-import-export-testkit-20251112-023402/` - Import/export test data

**Status:**
- ✅ **Automation RFC** extracted and integrated into `docs/technical/automation_system.md`
  - Complete automation engine specification (~7,500 words)
  - Trigger → Condition → Action model
  - JSON schemas, safety mechanisms, example automations
- ✅ **Import/Export specs** extracted and integrated into `docs/technical/import_export_spec.md`
  - Complete data portability specification (~6,000 words)
  - Export formats (.brain, CSV, ICS)
  - Import formats (Trello, Asana, Generic CSV, ICS)
  - Dry-run preview system
  - Test data preserved in archive for reference

**Why Archived:**
These brainstorming sessions were valuable for extracting technical specifications, but the content is now properly integrated into official documentation. The files are preserved here for historical reference and to track the evolution of ideas.

---

### `claude-exploration/`

**Source:** `.notes/Claude (Inspiration Reference Only)`

**Contents:** Claude AI exploration sessions exploring alternative product visions (October-November 2025)

**Files:**
- 12 comprehensive exploration documents covering:
  - Personal Knowledge Management (PKM) vision
  - Note-taking and AI features
  - Graph view and advanced search
  - Real-time collaboration
  - Visual planning tools
  - Alternative feature prioritizations

**CRITICAL CONTEXT:**
⚠️ **These files represent a DIFFERENT product vision than the current direction.**

**Current Vision (docs/):**
- All-in-one productivity dashboard
- Habit tracking + time tracking + tasks + calendar + gamification
- Beautiful bento-card UI
- Privacy-first, local-first architecture
- Q1 2026 focus: IndexedDB migration, Notes MVP, File System Access API

**Claude Exploration Vision (archived):**
- Personal Knowledge Management (PKM) focus
- Note-taking with AI features as primary value proposition
- Graph view, backlinks, advanced search as core features
- Different feature prioritization and roadmap

**Why These Visions Differ:**
During exploration sessions, Claude investigated alternative approaches to building a "Second Brain" product. While the PKM/notes-first approach is valid and explored in depth, the current product direction focuses on productivity dashboard with habit tracking as the primary differentiator.

**Status:**
- ❌ **NOT integrated** into current docs (represents alternative product direction)
- ℹ️ **Preserved for reference** - these explorations contain valuable insights about PKM features, AI integration, and graph databases that may be relevant for future feature development (Q2-Q4 2026)

**Valuable Content to Consider Later:**
- Graph view architecture (Cytoscape.js, D3.js, force-directed layouts)
- Advanced search with semantic search and fuzzy matching
- AI features (auto-tagging, smart suggestions, content generation)
- Backlink system design
- Real-time collaboration architecture
- Visual planning tools (mind maps, flowcharts)

**Why Archived:**
These explorations were valuable for understanding different product approaches, but they don't align with the current product vision. They're preserved here in case we want to revisit PKM features in the future (e.g., Q3-Q4 2026 after core dashboard is mature).

---

### `superseded-docs/`

**Source:** `.notes/Official Documentation`

**Contents:** Old official documentation (pre-November 2025 reorganization)

**Files:**
- Old competitive analysis
- Old feature specifications (Gantt charts, time tracking)
- Outdated Firebase/Google Drive architecture docs
- Historical roadmaps and feature lists

**Status:**
- ✅ **Competitive analysis** - Detailed competitor feature matrices extracted and integrated into `docs/strategy/competitive_landscape.md`
  - Added executive summary with market gap analysis
  - Added complete feature comparison matrices (20 features × 5 competitors)
  - Added habit & productivity apps comparison
  - Added competitive scoring, positioning statements, threat analysis
  - Enhanced from 217 lines to ~375 lines
- ⚠️ **Firebase/Google Drive docs** - OUTDATED (Firebase removed in Sprint 2, Week 3-4)
- ℹ️ **Feature specs** - Some specs (Gantt, time tracking) may have valuable details not yet in current docs
  - Consider extracting to `docs/technical/` if implementing these features

**Why Archived:**
These were the official docs before the November 2025 comprehensive documentation rewrite. The competitive analysis had valuable detailed competitor matrices that were integrated into current docs. Other content is outdated (Firebase) or may be valuable for future feature implementation.

---

### `examples/`

**Source:** `.notes/Examples`

**Contents:** Example files, test data, and reference materials

**Files:**
- Sample task exports
- Example automation rules
- Reference screenshots
- Test data sets

**Status:**
- ℹ️ **Preserved as-is** - Examples are useful for testing and reference

**Why Archived:**
These examples are valuable for testing import/export functionality and for reference when implementing features. They're not part of official documentation but are useful to keep around.

---

## What Was Extracted and Integrated

### From ChatGPT Brainstorming:

**Created `docs/technical/automation_system.md`** (~7,500 words)
- Complete automation engine specification
- Data model with TypeScript interfaces and JSON Schema
- Trigger types: onCreate, onUpdate, onMove, onDue, cron
- Action types: set, move, addTag, create, notify, snooze
- Safety mechanisms: loop prevention, rate limiting, idempotency
- IndexedDB storage and Service Worker integration
- 5 detailed example automations

**Created `docs/technical/import_export_spec.md`** (~6,000 words)
- Complete data portability specification
- Export formats: JSON Bundle (.brain), CSV, ICS
- Import formats: .brain, Trello JSON, Asana CSV, Generic CSV, ICS
- Dry-run preview system with conflict resolution
- Field mapping UI specification
- Lossless round-trip guarantees

### From Superseded Docs:

**Enhanced `docs/strategy/competitive_landscape.md`** (~375 lines, enhanced from 217)
- Added executive summary with market gap analysis
- Added complete feature comparison matrices (All-in-One platforms)
- Added habit & productivity apps comparison table
- Added competitive scoring summary (current 4.5/10 → target 9.5/10 by Q4 2026)
- Added strategic recommendations by sprint
- Added positioning statements for marketing
- Added threat analysis (High/Medium/Low threats with mitigations)

### From Claude Exploration:

**Nothing integrated** - Represents alternative product vision (PKM/notes-first vs current productivity dashboard)
- Preserved for future reference when implementing:
  - Graph view (Q3 2026)
  - Advanced search (Q3 2026)
  - AI features (Q4 2026+)
  - Real-time collaboration (Q2 2026)

---

## How to Use This Archive

### When to Reference Archive Files:

1. **Implementing automations** → Check `chatgpt-brainstorming/neuman-brain-automations-rfc-*`
2. **Implementing import/export** → Check test data in `chatgpt-brainstorming/neuman-brain-import-export-testkit-*`
3. **Implementing PKM features** (Q3-Q4 2026) → Check `claude-exploration/` for graph view, AI features
4. **Competitive analysis deep-dive** → Check `superseded-docs/` for detailed competitor feature matrices (already integrated into current docs)
5. **Testing with example data** → Check `examples/`

### When NOT to Use Archive Files:

1. **Looking for current project information** → Always use `docs/` first
2. **Understanding current product vision** → Use `docs/product/product_vision.md` (NOT Claude exploration files)
3. **Current architecture** → Use `docs/technical/` (NOT superseded Firebase docs)
4. **Current roadmap** → Use `docs/product/feature_roadmap.md` (NOT archived roadmaps)

---

## File Preservation Philosophy

**Why archive instead of delete?**

1. **Historical context** - Track evolution of ideas and product decisions
2. **Future reference** - PKM features, AI integration, graph databases may be relevant in Q3-Q4 2026
3. **Test data** - Import/export test data valuable for implementation
4. **Learning** - See what approaches were explored and why they weren't chosen
5. **Reversibility** - If we change direction, we have the context

**Storage cost:** Minimal (~2MB total) - archive costs are negligible compared to value of historical context

---

## Maintenance

### When to Add to Archive:

- Old official docs that are superseded by new comprehensive docs
- Brainstorming sessions that produce extracted specifications
- Alternative product vision explorations
- Historical roadmaps and feature lists (after major pivots)

### When to Clean Archive:

- **Never delete without review** - storage is cheap, context is valuable
- Consider moving to `archive/deprecated/` if truly obsolete
- Update this README when adding new categories

### Archive Organization:

```
.archive/
├── README.md                          # This file
├── gemini-analysis-2025-11-19/       # Gemini codebase analysis (guides extracted to docs/technical/)
├── chatgpt-brainstorming/            # ChatGPT RFC specs (extracted to docs/technical/)
├── claude-exploration/               # Alternative PKM vision (preserved for Q3-Q4 2026)
├── superseded-docs/                  # Old official docs (competitive analysis extracted)
└── examples/                         # Test data and reference materials
```

---

## Questions?

- **"Should I reference archive files?"** → Only for historical context or future features (PKM, AI)
- **"Where's the current info?"** → Always check `docs/` first
- **"Why keep Claude exploration files?"** → Valuable for Q3-Q4 2026 PKM features, even though not current direction
- **"Can I delete .notes/ now?"** → Yes, all content copied to .archive/

---

**Last Review:** November 19, 2025 (Task cleanup: 59 completed tasks archived, 44 active tasks remain)
**Next Review:** When adding major new archive content or implementing archived features (Q2-Q4 2026)

**Enhanced `docs/strategy/competitive_landscape.md`** (~375 lines, enhanced from 217)
- Added executive summary with market gap analysis
- Added complete feature comparison matrices (All-in-One platforms)
- Added habit & productivity apps comparison table
- Added competitive scoring summary (current 4.5/10 → target 9.5/10 by Q4 2026)
- Added strategic recommendations by sprint
- Added positioning statements for marketing
- Added threat analysis (High/Medium/Low threats with mitigations)

### From Claude Exploration:

**Nothing integrated** - Represents alternative product vision (PKM/notes-first vs current productivity dashboard)
- Preserved for future reference when implementing:
  - Graph view (Q3 2026)
  - Advanced search (Q3 2026)
  - AI features (Q4 2026+)
  - Real-time collaboration (Q2 2026)

---

## How to Use This Archive

### When to Reference Archive Files:

1. **Implementing automations** → Check `chatgpt-brainstorming/neuman-brain-automations-rfc-*`
2. **Implementing import/export** → Check test data in `chatgpt-brainstorming/neuman-brain-import-export-testkit-*`
3. **Implementing PKM features** (Q3-Q4 2026) → Check `claude-exploration/` for graph view, AI features
4. **Competitive analysis deep-dive** → Check `superseded-docs/` for detailed competitor feature matrices (already integrated into current docs)
5. **Testing with example data** → Check `examples/`

### When NOT to Use Archive Files:

1. **Looking for current project information** → Always use `docs/` first
2. **Understanding current product vision** → Use `docs/product/product_vision.md` (NOT Claude exploration files)
3. **Current architecture** → Use `docs/technical/` (NOT superseded Firebase docs)
4. **Current roadmap** → Use `docs/product/feature_roadmap.md` (NOT archived roadmaps)

---

## File Preservation Philosophy

**Why archive instead of delete?**

1. **Historical context** - Track evolution of ideas and product decisions
2. **Future reference** - PKM features, AI integration, graph databases may be relevant in Q3-Q4 2026
3. **Test data** - Import/export test data valuable for implementation
4. **Learning** - See what approaches were explored and why they weren't chosen
5. **Reversibility** - If we change direction, we have the context

**Storage cost:** Minimal (~2MB total) - archive costs are negligible compared to value of historical context

---

## Maintenance

### When to Add to Archive:

- Old official docs that are superseded by new comprehensive docs
- Brainstorming sessions that produce extracted specifications
- Alternative product vision explorations
- Historical roadmaps and feature lists (after major pivots)

### When to Clean Archive:

- **Never delete without review** - storage is cheap, context is valuable
- Consider moving to `archive/deprecated/` if truly obsolete
- Update this README when adding new categories

### Archive Organization:

```
.archive/
├── README.md                          # This file
├── gemini-analysis-2025-11-19/       # Gemini codebase analysis (guides extracted to docs/technical/)
├── chatgpt-brainstorming/            # ChatGPT RFC specs (extracted to docs/technical/)
├── claude-exploration/               # Alternative PKM vision (preserved for Q3-Q4 2026)
├── superseded-docs/                  # Old official docs (competitive analysis extracted)
└── examples/                         # Test data and reference materials
```

---

## Questions?

- **"Should I reference archive files?"** → Only for historical context or future features (PKM, AI)
- **"Where's the current info?"** → Always check `docs/` first
- **"Why keep Claude exploration files?"** → Valuable for Q3-Q4 2026 PKM features, even though not current direction
- **"Can I delete .notes/ now?"** → Yes, all content copied to .archive/

---

    86→├── index.ts              # Public API exports
    87→├── formatting.ts         # formatDate, formatTime, formatDuration
    88→├── duration.ts           # Duration calculations and parsing
    89→├── arithmetic.ts         # Date math (add/subtract days, months)
    90→└── relative.ts           # Relative time formatting ("2 hours ago")
    91→```
    92→
    93→### Search Utility Structure
    94→
    95→```typescript
    96→// src/utils/search.ts
    97→
    98→interface SearchOptions {
    99→  caseSensitive?: boolean;
   100→  fuzzy?: boolean;
   101→  threshold?: number; // Fuzzy match threshold (0-1)
   102→}
   103→
   104→function createSearchMatcher(
   105→  query: string,
   106→  options?: SearchOptions
   107→): (text: string) => boolean
   108→
   109→function searchMultipleFields<T>(
   110→  items: T[],
   111→  fields: (keyof T)[],
   112→  query: string,
   113→  options?: SearchOptions
   114→): T[]
   115→
   116→function filterByTags<T extends { tags: string[] }>(
   117→  items: T[],
   118→  tags: string[]
   119→): T[]
   120→
   121→function highlightMatches(
   122→  text: string,
   123→  query: string
   124→): string // Returns HTML with <mark> tags
   125→```

   217→## Technical Details
   218→
   219→### Calendar Item Adapters
   220→
   221→**Event Adapter:**
   222→```typescript
   223→class EventAdapter implements CalendarDataAdapter<CalendarEvent> {
   224→  adapt(events: CalendarEvent[]): CalendarItem[] {
   225→    return events.map(event => ({
   226→      id: event.id,
   227→      date: new Date(event.date),
   228→      title: event.title,
   229→      color: 'magenta', // Default event color
   230→      timeRange: event.isAllDay ? null : {
   231→        start: event.startTime,
   232→        end: event.endTime
   233→      },
   234→      type: 'event',
   235→      metadata: event
   236→    }));
   237→  }
   238→
   239→  getColor(event: CalendarEvent): string {
   240→    return 'magenta'; // Or event.category color
   241→  }
   242→
   243→  onClick(event: CalendarEvent): void {
   244→    // Open event edit modal
   245→  }
   246→}
   247→```
   248→
   249→**Time Entry Adapter:**
   250→```typescript
   251→class TimeEntryAdapter implements CalendarDataAdapter<TimeEntry> {
   252→  constructor(private projects: Map<string, Project>) {}
   253→
   254→  adapt(entries: TimeEntry[]): CalendarItem[] {
   255→    return entries.map(entry => {
   256→      const project = this.projects.get(entry.projectId);
   257→      return {
   258→        id: entry.id,
   259→        date: new Date(entry.startTime),
   260→        title: entry.description,
   261→        color: project?.color,
   262→        timeRange: {
   263→          start: formatTime(new Date(entry.startTime)),
   264→          end: entry.endTime ? formatTime(new Date(entry.endTime)) : 'Running'
   265→        },
   266→        type: 'time-entry',
   267→        metadata: entry
   268→      };
   269→    });
   270→  }
   271→
   272→  getColor(entry: TimeEntry): string {
   273→    const project = this.projects.get(entry.projectId);
   274→    return project?.color || '#6B7280'; // Gray fallback
   275→  }
   276→
   277→  onClick(entry: TimeEntry): void {
   278→    // Open EditTimeEntryModal
   279→  }
   280→}
   281→```
   282→
   283→### DateTime Migration Example
   284→
   285→**Before:**
   286→```typescript
   287→import { formatDuration } from '../utils/timeFormatters';
   288→import { getStandardDateKey, isToday } from '../utils/dateUtils';
   289→```
   290→
   291→**After:**
   292→```typescript
   293→import { formatDuration, formatDate, isToday } from '../utils/datetime';
   294→```
   295→
   296→### Search Migration Example
   297→
   298→**Before (in useNotesStore):**
   299→```typescript
   300→searchNotes: (query) => {
   301→  return get().notes.filter(note =>
   302→    note.title.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase()) ||
   303→    note.content.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase()) ||
   304→    note.tags.some(tag => tag.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase()))
   305→  );
   306→}
   307→```
   308→
   309→**After:**
   310→```typescript
   311→import { searchMultipleFields, filterByTags } from '../utils/search';
   312→
   313→searchNotes: (query) => {
   314→  const notes = get().notes;
   315→  if (!query) return notes;
   316→
   317→  return searchMultipleFields(notes, ['title', 'content'], query);
   318→}
   319→```

# Task: Phase 3.7 - Calendar Consolidation & Rename

**Created:** 2025-01-22
**Status:** In Progress
**Priority:** P0 (Architecture Foundation)
**Complexity:** Medium
**Estimated Effort:** 2-3 weeks
**New Page Name:** "Time & Planning"

---

## Context

Phase 3 (Time Tracking) is complete with 71% Toggl parity. Before implementing Phase 4 (Billing Features), we need to consolidate the duplicate calendar systems.

**User Direction:**
- TimeEntryCalendar looks WAY better than Planning Calendar Widget
- Add Planning features (events, recurring, multi-day) to TimeEntryCalendar
- Replace Planning page entirely with enhanced Time Tracking
- Rename to **"Time & Planning"** to show dual purpose

**Strategy:** Extend TimeEntryCalendar with event functionality, delete old Planning Calendar Widget.

---

## Scope

### Phase 3.7A: Enhance TimeEntryCalendar with Events (Week 1-2)
**Goal:** Add event creation, recurring events, multi-day support to beautiful TimeEntryCalendar

### Phase 3.7B: Replace Planning Page (Week 2-3)
**Goal:** Rename Time Tracking → "Time & Planning", delete old Planning page

### Phase 3.7C: Cleanup & Optimization (Week 3)
**Goal:** Remove duplicate Calendar Widget code, optimize bundle size

---

## Architecture Plan (SIMPLIFIED)

### Enhanced TimeEntryCalendar

**Keep:** Beautiful TimeEntryCalendar as foundation (src/components/TimeEntryCalendar.tsx)

**Add:**
```
src/components/
├── TimeEntryCalendar.tsx        # ENHANCE with event support
├── EventCreateModal.tsx         # NEW - Create/edit events
├── RecurringEventModal.tsx      # NEW - Configure recurrence
└── EventBlock.tsx               # NEW - Render event blocks
```

**Strategy:** Extend existing TimeEntryCalendar to render BOTH time entries AND events
- Time entries: Project-colored blocks (existing)
- Events: Magenta/blue bars (NEW)
- Visual distinction clear
- Unified navigation (existing)
- Click day → choose "Add Event" or "Add Time Entry"

### Data Adapter Pattern

```typescript
interface CalendarItem {
  id: string;
  date: Date;
  title: string;
  color?: string;
  timeRange?: { start: string; end: string };
  type: 'event' | 'time-entry' | 'task';
  metadata: any; // Original item reference
}

interface CalendarDataAdapter<T> {
  adapt(items: T[]): CalendarItem[];
  getColor(item: T): string;
  getTimeRange(item: T): { start: string; end: string } | null;
  onClick(item: T): void;
}
```

### DateTime Module Structure

```
src/utils/datetime/
├── index.ts              # Public API exports
├── formatting.ts         # formatDate, formatTime, formatDuration
├── duration.ts           # Duration calculations and parsing
├── arithmetic.ts         # Date math (add/subtract days, months)
└── relative.ts           # Relative time formatting ("2 hours ago")
```

### Search Utility Structure

```typescript
// src/utils/search.ts

interface SearchOptions {
  caseSensitive?: boolean;
  fuzzy?: boolean;
  threshold?: number; // Fuzzy match threshold (0-1)
}

function createSearchMatcher(
  query: string,
  options?: SearchOptions
): (text: string) => boolean

function searchMultipleFields<T>(
  items: T[],
  fields: (keyof T)[],
  query: string,
  options?: SearchOptions
): T[]

function filterByTags<T extends { tags: string[] }>(
  items: T[],
  tags: string[]
): T[]

function highlightMatches(
  text: string,
  query: string
): string // Returns HTML with <mark> tags
```

---

## Implementation Checklist

### Phase 3.7A: Calendar Consolidation

#### Week 1: Core Architecture
- [ ] Create `src/components/calendar/` directory structure
- [ ] Define `CalendarItem` and `CalendarDataAdapter` interfaces
- [ ] Implement `CalendarCore.tsx` base component
- [ ] Create `EventAdapter.ts` (migrate from Calendar Widget)
- [ ] Create `TimeEntryAdapter.ts` (migrate from TimeEntryCalendar)
- [ ] Implement `MonthView.tsx` (unified month grid)
- [ ] Implement `WeekView.tsx` (unified week timeline)
- [ ] Implement `DayView.tsx` (unified day schedule)
- [ ] Implement `AgendaView.tsx` (unified list view)
- [ ] Create renderer components (EventBlock, TimeEntryBlock)

#### Week 2: Integration & Migration
- [ ] Build `UnifiedCalendar.tsx` orchestrator
- [ ] Add view mode switching (Month/Week/Day/Agenda)
- [ ] Add data type filtering (show/hide events/time/tasks)
- [ ] Implement unified navigation (prev/next/today)
- [ ] Add project legend for time entries
- [ ] Migrate Calendar Widget to use CalendarCore
- [ ] Update TimeTracking page to use UnifiedCalendar

## Technical Details

### Calendar Item Adapters

**Event Adapter:**
```typescript
class EventAdapter implements CalendarDataAdapter<CalendarEvent> {
  adapt(events: CalendarEvent[]): CalendarItem[] {
    return events.map(event => ({
      id: event.id,
      date: new Date(event.date),
      title: event.title,
      color: 'magenta', // Default event color
      timeRange: event.isAllDay ? null : {
        start: event.startTime,
        end: event.endTime
      },
      type: 'event',
      metadata: event
    }));
  }

  getColor(event: CalendarEvent): string {
    return 'magenta'; // Or event.category color
  }

  onClick(event: CalendarEvent): void {
    // Open event edit modal
  }
}
```

**Time Entry Adapter:**
```typescript
class TimeEntryAdapter implements CalendarDataAdapter<TimeEntry> {
  constructor(private projects: Map<string, Project>) {}

  adapt(entries: TimeEntry[]): CalendarItem[] {
    return entries.map(entry => {
      const project = this.projects.get(entry.projectId);
      return {
        id: entry.id,
        date: new Date(entry.startTime),
        title: entry.description,
        color: project?.color,
        timeRange: {
          start: formatTime(new Date(entry.startTime)),
          end: entry.endTime ? formatTime(new Date(entry.endTime)) : 'Running'
        },
        type: 'time-entry',
        metadata: entry
      };
    });
  }

  getColor(entry: TimeEntry): string {
    const project = this.projects.get(entry.projectId);
    return project?.color || '#6B7280'; // Gray fallback
  }

  onClick(entry: TimeEntry): void {
    // Open EditTimeEntryModal
  }
}
```

### DateTime Migration Example

**Before:**
```typescript
import { formatDuration } from '../utils/timeFormatters';
import { getStandardDateKey, isToday } from '../utils/dateUtils';
```

**After:**
```typescript
import { formatDuration, formatDate, isToday } from '../utils/datetime';
```

### Search Migration Example

**Before (in useNotesStore):**
```typescript
searchNotes: (query) => {
  return get().notes.filter(note =>
    note.title.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase()) ||
    note.content.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase()) ||
    note.tags.some(tag => tag.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase()))
  );
}
```

**After:**
```typescript
import { searchMultipleFields, filterByTags } from '../utils/search';

searchNotes: (query) => {
  const notes = get().notes;
  if (!query) return notes;

  return searchMultipleFields(notes, ['title', 'content'], query);
}
```

## Migration Strategy

### Calendar Migration (Zero-Downtime)

**Phase 1: Parallel Implementation**
1. Build new UnifiedCalendar alongside existing components
2. Add feature flag: `USE_UNIFIED_CALENDAR` (default: false)
3. Test in isolation with mock data

**Phase 2: Beta Rollout**
1. Set `USE_UNIFIED_CALENDAR = true`
2. Monitor for errors/regressions
3. Gather user feedback
4. Fix issues

**Phase 3: Deprecation**
1. Remove feature flag (always use unified)
2. Delete old TimeEntryCalendar component
3. Refactor Calendar Widget to thin wrapper
4. Clean up imports

### DateTime Migration (Direct Replacement)
1. Create new module with ALL functions
2. Update one directory at a time (components/, widgets/, stores/)
3. Run TypeScript type-check after each directory
4. Delete old modules when all imports updated

### Search Migration (Store by Store)
1. Create utility with all search functions
2. Update useNotesStore (test thoroughly)
3. Update useTimeTrackingStore (test thoroughly)
4. Update useKanbanStore (test thoroughly)

## Risks & Mitigation

| Risk | Impact | Probability | Mitigation |
|------|--------|-------------|------------|
| **Calendar regression** | High | Medium | Feature flag, beta period, comprehensive testing |
| **Performance degradation** | Medium | Low | Virtual scrolling, memoization, lazy loading |
| **Import errors during migration** | Medium | Medium | TypeScript type-check after each batch |
| **User confusion (unified calendar)** | Low | Low | Clear visual distinction (colors, icons) |
| **DateTime edge cases** | Medium | Low | Unit tests, timezone handling, leap year tests |

**Issue:** Calendar tab showed blank page with console error: "Rendered more hooks than during the previous render"

**Fix:** Moved `expandedEvents` useMemo (lines 73-104) to BEFORE the loading early return (line 106)

---

## Review Section

**Status:** ✅ COMPLETE
**Completion Date:** 2025-11-24
**Actual Time:** 2 weeks (as estimated)

### Summary of Achievements

**Phase 3.7 successfully consolidated duplicate calendar systems into single unified TimeEntryCalendar.**

### What Was Completed

**Week 1: Calendar Enhancement** ✅
- Enhanced TimeEntryCalendar with full event support
- Events (magenta bars) + Time Entries (project colors) coexist beautifully
- Created EventCreateModal for event creation/editing
- Implemented recurring events (daily/weekly/monthly/yearly)

# Task: Phase 3.7 - Calendar Consolidation & Rename

**Created:** 2025-01-22
**Status:** In Progress
**Priority:** P0 (Architecture Foundation)
**Complexity:** Medium
**Estimated Effort:** 2-3 weeks
**New Page Name:** "Time & Planning"

---

## Context

Phase 3 (Time Tracking) is complete with 71% Toggl parity. Before implementing Phase 4 (Billing Features), we need to consolidate the duplicate calendar systems.

**User Direction:**
- TimeEntryCalendar looks WAY better than Planning Calendar Widget
- Add Planning features (events, recurring, multi-day) to TimeEntryCalendar
- Replace Planning page entirely with enhanced Time Tracking
- Rename to **"Time & Planning"** to show dual purpose

**Strategy:** Extend TimeEntryCalendar with event functionality, delete old Planning Calendar Widget.

---

## Scope

### Phase 3.7A: Enhance TimeEntryCalendar with Events (Week 1-2)
**Goal:** Add event creation, recurring events, multi-day support to beautiful TimeEntryCalendar

### Phase 3.7B: Replace Planning Page (Week 2-3)
**Goal:** Rename Time Tracking → "Time & Planning", delete old Planning page

### Phase 3.7C: Cleanup & Optimization (Week 3)
**Goal:** Remove duplicate Calendar Widget code, optimize bundle size

---

## Architecture Plan (SIMPLIFIED)

### Enhanced TimeEntryCalendar

**Keep:** Beautiful TimeEntryCalendar as foundation (src/components/TimeEntryCalendar.tsx)

**Add:**
```
src/components/
├── TimeEntryCalendar.tsx        # ENHANCE with event support
├── EventCreateModal.tsx         # NEW - Create/edit events
├── RecurringEventModal.tsx      # NEW - Configure recurrence
└── EventBlock.tsx               # NEW - Render event blocks
```

**Strategy:** Extend existing TimeEntryCalendar to render BOTH time entries AND events
- Time entries: Project-colored blocks (existing)
- Events: Magenta/blue bars (NEW)
- Visual distinction clear
- Unified navigation (existing)
- Click day → choose "Add Event" or "Add Time Entry"

### Data Adapter Pattern

```typescript
interface CalendarItem {
  id: string;
  date: Date;
  title: string;
  color?: string;
  timeRange?: { start: string; end: string };
  type: 'event' | 'time-entry' | 'task';
  metadata: any; // Original item reference
}

interface CalendarDataAdapter<T> {
  adapt(items: T[]): CalendarItem[];
  getColor(item: T): string;
  getTimeRange(item: T): { start: string; end: string } | null;
  onClick(item: T): void;
}
```

### DateTime Module Structure

```
src/utils/datetime/
├── index.ts              # Public API exports
├── formatting.ts         # formatDate, formatTime, formatDuration
├── duration.ts           # Duration calculations and parsing
├── arithmetic.ts         # Date math (add/subtract days, months)
└── relative.ts           # Relative time formatting ("2 hours ago")
```

### Search Utility Structure

```typescript
// src/utils/search.ts

interface SearchOptions {
  caseSensitive?: boolean;
  fuzzy?: boolean;
  threshold?: number; // Fuzzy match threshold (0-1)
}

function createSearchMatcher(
  query: string,
  options?: SearchOptions
): (text: string) => boolean

function searchMultipleFields<T>(
  items: T[],
  fields: (keyof T)[],
  query: string,
  options?: SearchOptions
): T[]

function filterByTags<T extends { tags: string[] }>(
  items: T[],
  tags: string[]
): T[]

function highlightMatches(
  text: string,
  query: string
): string // Returns HTML with <mark> tags
```

---

## Implementation Checklist

### Phase 3.7A: Calendar Consolidation

#### Week 1: Core Architecture
- [ ] Create `src/components/calendar/` directory structure
- [ ] Define `CalendarItem` and `CalendarDataAdapter` interfaces
- [ ] Implement `CalendarCore.tsx` base component
- [ ] Create `EventAdapter.ts` (migrate from Calendar Widget)
- [ ] Create `TimeEntryAdapter.ts` (migrate from TimeEntryCalendar)
- [ ] Implement `MonthView.tsx` (unified month grid)
- [ ] Implement `WeekView.tsx` (unified week timeline)
- [ ] Implement `DayView.tsx` (unified day schedule)
- [ ] Implement `AgendaView.tsx` (unified list view)
- [ ] Create renderer components (EventBlock, TimeEntryBlock)

#### Week 2: Integration & Migration
- [ ] Build `UnifiedCalendar.tsx` orchestrator
- [ ] Add view mode switching (Month/Week/Day/Agenda)
- [ ] Add data type filtering (show/hide events/time/tasks)
- [ ] Implement unified navigation (prev/next/today)
- [ ] Add project legend for time entries
- [ ] Migrate Calendar Widget to use CalendarCore
- [ ] Update TimeTracking page to use UnifiedCalendar
- [ ] Add feature flag: `USE_UNIFIED_CALENDAR`
- [ ] Dark mode verification
- [ ] Performance testing (100+ items per month)
- [ ] Manual testing across all views
- [ ] Beta testing period (3-5 days)

#### Week 2.5: Cleanup
- [ ] Deprecate old `TimeEntryCalendar.tsx`
- [ ] Remove duplicate code
- [ ] Update documentation
- [ ] Bundle size verification

### Phase 3.7B: DateTime Utils Consolidation

- [ ] Create `src/utils/datetime/` directory
- [ ] Implement `formatting.ts`:
  - [ ] `formatDate(date, format)` - Short, long, ISO formats
  - [ ] `formatTime(date, use24Hour)` - 12h/24h time
  - [ ] `formatTimeRange(start, end)` - "9:00 AM - 5:00 PM"
  - [ ] `formatDateTime(date, format)` - Combined date + time
- [ ] Implement `duration.ts`:
  - [ ] `formatDuration(seconds, format)` - "2h 30m", "02:30:00"
  - [ ] `parseDuration(string)` - "2h 30m" → 9000 seconds
  - [ ] `calculateDuration(start, end)` - Seconds between dates
- [ ] Implement `arithmetic.ts`:
  - [ ] `addDays(date, days)`, `addMonths()`, `addYears()`
  - [ ] `subtractDays()`, `subtractMonths()`, `subtractYears()`
  - [ ] `startOfDay()`, `endOfDay()`, `startOfMonth()`, etc.
  - [ ] `getDaysInMonth(year, month)`
  - [ ] `isDateBetween(date, start, end)`
- [ ] Implement `relative.ts`:
  - [ ] `formatRelative(date)` - "2 hours ago", "in 3 days"
  - [ ] `formatRelativeShort(date)` - "2h ago", "3d"
- [ ] Create `index.ts` with public API
- [ ] Update imports in all components (20+ files):
  - [ ] Calendar components
  - [ ] Time Tracking components
  - [ ] Notes components (timestamps)
  - [ ] Task components (due dates)
  - [ ] Widgets (recent activity, etc.)
- [ ] Remove old `dateUtils.ts` and `timeFormatters.ts`
- [ ] Add unit tests

### Phase 3.7C: Search Utility Consolidation

- [ ] Create `src/utils/search.ts`
- [ ] Implement `createSearchMatcher(query, options)`
- [ ] Implement `searchMultipleFields(items, fields, query)`
- [ ] Implement `filterByTags(items, tags)`
- [ ] Implement `highlightMatches(text, query)`
- [ ] Update `useNotesStore`:
  - [ ] Replace inline search with `searchMultipleFields`
  - [ ] Add search highlighting in results
- [ ] Update `useTimeTrackingStore`:
  - [ ] Replace inline search with `searchMultipleFields`
  - [ ] Add search highlighting
- [ ] Update `useKanbanStore`:
  - [ ] Replace inline filter with `searchMultipleFields`
  - [ ] Add search highlighting in task cards
- [ ] Add fuzzy search option (optional, nice-to-have)
- [ ] Add unit tests

---

## Technical Details

### Calendar Item Adapters

**Event Adapter:**
```typescript
class EventAdapter implements CalendarDataAdapter<CalendarEvent> {
  adapt(events: CalendarEvent[]): CalendarItem[] {
    return events.map(event => ({
      id: event.id,
      date: new Date(event.date),
      title: event.title,
      color: 'magenta', // Default event color
      timeRange: event.isAllDay ? null : {
        start: event.startTime,
        end: event.endTime
      },
      type: 'event',
      metadata: event
    }));
  }

  getColor(event: CalendarEvent): string {
    return 'magenta'; // Or event.category color
  }

  onClick(event: CalendarEvent): void {
    // Open event edit modal
  }
}
```

**Time Entry Adapter:**
```typescript
class TimeEntryAdapter implements CalendarDataAdapter<TimeEntry> {
  constructor(private projects: Map<string, Project>) {}

  adapt(entries: TimeEntry[]): CalendarItem[] {
    return entries.map(entry => {
      const project = this.projects.get(entry.projectId);
      return {
        id: entry.id,
        date: new Date(entry.startTime),
        title: entry.description,
        color: project?.color,
        timeRange: {
          start: formatTime(new Date(entry.startTime)),
          end: entry.endTime ? formatTime(new Date(entry.endTime)) : 'Running'
        },
        type: 'time-entry',
        metadata: entry
      };
    });
  }

  getColor(entry: TimeEntry): string {
    const project = this.projects.get(entry.projectId);
    return project?.color || '#6B7280'; // Gray fallback
  }

  onClick(entry: TimeEntry): void {
    // Open EditTimeEntryModal
  }
}
```

### DateTime Migration Example

**Before:**
```typescript
import { formatDuration } from '../utils/timeFormatters';
import { getStandardDateKey, isToday } from '../utils/dateUtils';
```

**After:**
```typescript
import { formatDuration, formatDate, isToday } from '../utils/datetime';
```

### Search Migration Example

**Before (in useNotesStore):**
```typescript
searchNotes: (query) => {
  return get().notes.filter(note =>
    note.title.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase()) ||
    note.content.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase()) ||
    note.tags.some(tag => tag.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase()))
  );
}
```

**After:**
```typescript
import { searchMultipleFields, filterByTags } from '../utils/search';

searchNotes: (query) => {
  const notes = get().notes;
  if (!query) return notes;

  return searchMultipleFields(notes, ['title', 'content'], query);
}
```

---

## Migration Strategy

### Calendar Migration (Zero-Downtime)

**Phase 1: Parallel Implementation**
1. Build new UnifiedCalendar alongside existing components
2. Add feature flag: `USE_UNIFIED_CALENDAR` (default: false)
3. Test in isolation with mock data

**Phase 2: Beta Rollout**
1. Set `USE_UNIFIED_CALENDAR = true`
2. Monitor for errors/regressions
3. Gather user feedback
4. Fix issues

**Phase 3: Deprecation**
1. Remove feature flag (always use unified)
2. Delete old TimeEntryCalendar component
3. Refactor Calendar Widget to thin wrapper
4. Clean up imports

### DateTime Migration (Direct Replacement)
1. Create new module with ALL functions
2. Update one directory at a time (components/, widgets/, stores/)
3. Run TypeScript type-check after each directory
4. Delete old modules when all imports updated

### Search Migration (Store by Store)
1. Create utility with all search functions
2. Update useNotesStore (test thoroughly)
3. Update useTimeTrackingStore (test thoroughly)
4. Update useKanbanStore (test thoroughly)

---

## Success Criteria

### Calendar Consolidation
- [ ] Single calendar component renders events AND time entries
- [ ] All 4 view modes work (Month/Week/Day/Agenda)
- [ ] Click interactions preserved (edit events/entries)
- [ ] Project colors display correctly for time entries
- [ ] Performance: <100ms render for 30-day month with 100 items
- [ ] Code reduction: 1,854 → ~1,300 lines (30% reduction)
- [ ] Bundle size: <5KB increase (lazy loading)
- [ ] Zero regressions in Time Tracking workflows
- [ ] Zero regressions in Planning/Calendar workflows

### DateTime Consolidation
- [ ] All date/time operations use datetime module
- [ ] Zero import errors after migration
- [ ] Consistent formatting across all features
- [ ] Unit tests pass for edge cases (timezones, leap years, etc.)
- [ ] Code reduction: 300 → 200 lines (33% reduction)

### Search Consolidation
- [ ] Search works identically in Notes, Tasks, Time Tracking
- [ ] Search highlighting functional
- [ ] Performance: <50ms for 1000 items
- [ ] Tag filtering works across all features
- [ ] Code reduction: Remove 100+ lines of duplicate filter logic

---

## Risks & Mitigation

| Risk | Impact | Probability | Mitigation |
|------|--------|-------------|------------|
| **Calendar regression** | High | Medium | Feature flag, beta period, comprehensive testing |
| **Performance degradation** | Medium | Low | Virtual scrolling, memoization, lazy loading |
| **Import errors during migration** | Medium | Medium | TypeScript type-check after each batch |
| **User confusion (unified calendar)** | Low | Low | Clear visual distinction (colors, icons) |
| **DateTime edge cases** | Medium | Low | Unit tests, timezone handling, leap year tests |

---

## Files to Create

**Calendar System (15 files):**
- `src/components/calendar/UnifiedCalendar.tsx`
- `src/components/calendar/CalendarCore.tsx`
- `src/components/calendar/adapters/CalendarDataAdapter.ts`
- `src/components/calendar/adapters/EventAdapter.ts`
- `src/components/calendar/adapters/TimeEntryAdapter.ts`
- `src/components/calendar/adapters/TaskAdapter.ts`
- `src/components/calendar/views/MonthView.tsx`
- `src/components/calendar/views/WeekView.tsx`
- `src/components/calendar/views/DayView.tsx`
- `src/components/calendar/views/AgendaView.tsx`
- `src/components/calendar/renderers/EventBlock.tsx`
- `src/components/calendar/renderers/TimeEntryBlock.tsx`
- `src/components/calendar/renderers/TaskBar.tsx`
- `src/components/calendar/types.ts`
- `src/components/calendar/index.ts`

**DateTime Module (6 files):**
- `src/utils/datetime/index.ts`
- `src/utils/datetime/formatting.ts`
- `src/utils/datetime/duration.ts`
- `src/utils/datetime/arithmetic.ts`
- `src/utils/datetime/relative.ts`
- `src/utils/datetime/types.ts`

**Search Utility (1 file):**
- `src/utils/search.ts`

---

## Files to Modify

**Calendar Migration:**
- `src/pages/TimeTracking.tsx` (use UnifiedCalendar)
- `src/pages/Planning.tsx` (use UnifiedCalendar)
- `src/widgets/Calendar.tsx` (refactor to wrapper)

**DateTime Migration (~20 files):**
- All calendar components
- `src/components/TimeTrackingPanel.tsx`
- `src/components/TimeEntryList.tsx`
- `src/components/MonthlyTimeReport.tsx`
- `src/widgets/*.tsx` (various widgets using dates)
- All stores (`useTimeTrackingStore`, `useCalendarStore`, etc.)

**Search Migration:**
- `src/stores/useNotesStore.ts`
- `src/stores/useTimeTrackingStore.ts`
- `src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts`

**Cleanup:**
- DELETE: `src/components/TimeEntryCalendar.tsx` (after migration)
- DELETE: `src/utils/dateUtils.ts` (after migration)
- DELETE: `src/utils/timeFormatters.ts` (after migration)

---

## Progress Log

### 2025-01-22 - Task Created
- Comprehensive plan created
- Architecture designed (simplified approach per user direction)
- Files identified for creation/modification
- Ready for Week 1 implementation

### 2025-01-22 - Week 1 Implementation (Session 1)
- Enhanced TimeEntryCalendar with event rendering
- Created EventCreateModal component
- Integrated modal into Time Tracking page
- Renamed page to "Time & Planning"
- Events (magenta bars) + Time Entries (project colors) coexist beautifully
- Hover-to-add event button functional
- Recurring events (daily/weekly/monthly/yearly) supported
- Multi-day event support implemented
- TypeScript compilation passes
- Commits pushed to remote (78a0b5b, e5101c8)
- Week 1 - 100% complete ✅

### 2025-01-23 - Bug Fix: React Hooks Violation
**Issue:** Calendar tab showed blank page with console error: "Rendered more hooks than during the previous render"

**Root Cause:** `useMemo` hook was called AFTER early return when `loading=true`, violating Rules of Hooks

**Fix:** Moved `expandedEvents` useMemo (lines 73-104) to BEFORE the loading early return (line 106)

**Testing:**
- ✅ TypeScript compilation passes
- ✅ HMR update successful
- ✅ No console errors
- ✅ Calendar now loads correctly

### 2025-01-23 - Migration: ALL Calendar Widget Features
**Scope:** Migrate ALL functionality from Planning Calendar Widget to TimeEntryCalendar to preserve features

**Changes Made:**
1. ✅ Added view mode state (`monthly` | `weekly` | `daily` | `agenda`)
2. ✅ Added view mode switcher (4 segmented buttons: Month/Week/Day/List)
3. ✅ Moved "New Event" button to header (prominent, next to Today button)
4. ✅ Integrated WeekView component (shows 7-day timeline with time slots)
5. ✅ Integrated DayView component (shows 24-hour schedule)
6. ✅ Integrated AgendaView component (shows upcoming events list)
7. ✅ Updated navigation logic (month/week/day period handling)
8. ✅ Added Print button (window.print() on click)
9. ✅ Fixed TypeScript errors (removed unused variables)

**Features Now Available:**
- ✅ Month view with events + time entries
- ✅ Week view with time slots
- ✅ Day view with hourly timeline
- ✅ List/Agenda view for upcoming events
- ✅ Smart navigation (period changes based on view)
- ✅ "New Event" button in header (not tiny hover icon)
- ✅ Print calendar functionality
- ✅ All event types (recurring, multi-day, timed, all-day)
- ✅ Time entry integration across all views

**File Changes:**
- src/components/TimeEntryCalendar.tsx (390 → 553 lines)
  - Added imports for WeekView, DayView, AgendaView
  - Added viewMode state and switcher UI
